methods.description: This method describes the process of sampling and analyzing diatom assemblages and environmental parameters in salterns to understand the community structure and environmental conditions.

steps.description: A total of thirteen stations from two salterns were surveyed in Muan (site A) and Sinan (site B) in the present study.
steps.searchable_summary: survey 13 stations in Muan and Sinan salterns

steps.description: Samples were collected from the adjacent tidal flats, viz., outside of the salterns which were considered to be the reference sites.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from adjacent tidal flats

steps.description: Surface sediments (<3 cm) were sampled to analyze general sediment properties such as water content.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sediment properties from surface samples

steps.description: The top 0.5 cm of surface sediment was collected for the analysis of diatom assemblages.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze diatom assemblages from surface sediment

steps.description: Light microscope and scanning electron microscope were used to obtain photographic documentation of diatoms and/or to perform species identification.
steps.searchable_summary: use microscopes for diatom documentation and identification

methods.description: This method involves statistical analyses to understand the relative abundance, diversity, and environmental factors affecting diatom assemblages in salterns.

steps.description: The relative abundance of each taxon was calculated through counting diatom valves under the light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative abundance of diatom taxa

steps.description: Cluster analysis (CA) and non-metric multidimensional scaling were carried out on the resulting matrix.
steps.searchable_summary: perform cluster analysis and multidimensional scaling

steps.description: Bray-Curtis similarity coefficients were calculated, and the data were subjected to group average sorting.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Bray-Curtis similarity and perform group sorting

steps.description: The analysis of similarities (ANOSIM) test was performed to confirm that these groups differed significantly.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOSIM test for group differences

steps.description: Similarity percentages (SIMPER) analysis was performed to identify the taxa responsible for differences between groups.
steps.searchable_summary: perform SIMPER analysis to identify taxa differences

steps.description: Biota-environment (BIOENV) procedure was used to determine the factors determining the structure of diatom assemblages.
steps.searchable_summary: perform BIOENV procedure for environmental factors

steps.description: Permutational multivariate analysis of variance (PER-MANOVA) was performed to assess the significance of variables across sites and salinities.
Indicator value (IndVal) analysis was performed to identify indicator diatom species for specific ranges in the salinity groups.
steps.searchable_summary: perform IndVal analysis for indicator species
